analysis.consistency: The item description is internally consistent. The core magical effect (resistance to ranged weapon damage) is balanced by a significant mechanical drawback (the curse forcing target redirection).
analysis.quality: High quality. The item presents a clear, interesting, and powerful trade-off. Resistance to ranged damage is strong, but the forced targeting mechanic creates compelling roleplaying and tactical situations, especially in combat against archers or projectile users.

mechanics.primary_benefit: Resistance to damage from ranged weapon attacks.

curse_mechanic.activation_condition: A ranged weapon attack is made against a target within 10 feet of the wielder.
curse_mechanic.effect: The wielder automatically becomes the target instead.
curse_mechanic.removal_condition: The _remove curse_ spell or similar magic.

mechanics.clarifications_needed: None. The mechanics are clearly defined, especially the targeting redirection rule which overrides the original target selection.
